summary: FINAL E2E retest after iteration_65 UX fixes. All 3 frontend fixes VERIFIED WORKING. Steps 1-6 all PASS. Step 7 partial-pass (delta itself PASS: dirty-detection correctly fires exactly monitor-type + silence PUTs and SKIPS /snmp because community unchanged; however the PRE-EXISTING backend 404 on /monitor-type — unchanged since iteration_65 — still produces an error toast). Key measurements: (a) STEP 3: exactly 1 PUT fires on pure-silence save, to /silence with {silenced:true,reason:'Final E2E retest'} → 200 OK. No spurious PUT to /monitor-type or /snmp (dirty-detection fix confirmed). (b) STEP 4: silence-badge-192.168.1.3 appeared 0.51s after modal close (well under the 1s target — optimistic update confirmed, no page reload needed). (c) STEP 5: reopen modal → silence-toggle is CHECKED and silence-reason is pre-filled with 'Final E2E retest' (useEffect re-seed on device.alerts_silenced/reason confirmed). (d) STEP 6: single PUT /silence {silenced:false} → toast 'Alert RIATTIVATI per questo device' → badge disappears in 0.50s. (e) STEP 7: switched monitor_type SNMP→Ping + enabled silence simultaneously → exactly 2 PUTs fired (monitor-type + silence), /snmp correctly skipped because community not dirty. Cleanup performed: alerts_silenced=false on 192.168.1.3, monitor_type restored to SNMP (backend ignored the change because /monitor-type still 404s, so DB state never deviated — serendipitous).

minor.issue: Still returns 404 'Device non trovato' for auto-discovered device 192.168.1.3 (id=1089e659-...). Pre-existing bug carried over from iteration_65 — NOT in scope of this retest but continues to cause a red error toast whenever the user changes monitor_type. Needs the same multi-source resolver (managed_devices → db.devices → poll_<ip> synthetic + upsert) that was applied to /silence.
Same 404 pattern as /monitor-type (not exercised in this retest since the frontend now skips it when community unchanged, but would fail if user changes SNMP fields). Same fix needed.

action_items: Backend only: apply the same multi-source resolver to PUT /monitor-type and PUT /snmp in /app/backend/routes/connector.py (share the resolver helper already used by /silence). This removes the residual error toast in STEP 7 and unblocks users who genuinely want to change monitor method on auto-discovered devices. Not blocking for this iteration's delta but should be the next fix.
critical_code_review_comments: DeviceEditModal useEffect re-seed dependency list is correct: [device?.id, device?.alerts_silenced, device?.alerts_silenced_reason, device?.monitor_type, device?.snmp_version, device?.snmp_community]. Handles both same-device reopen and switch-to-another-device without unmount.

Dirty-detection in save() is sound for the current schema; guards all 3 PUTs. One subtle corner: if snmpVersion==="v3" and user only toggles a v3 password without changing version, snmpFieldsDirty correctly picks it up via the v3 sub-object comparison. Good.

Optimistic update in ClientOverviewPage.optimisticUpdateDevice uses setDevices(prev => prev.map(...)) keyed by id — correct React-18 functional update. The spread {...d, ...updatedDevice} preserves fields not present in the modal (ip_address, name, status) so the row doesn't flicker.

Minor: onSaved is currently called only on the all-success path (after `if (errors.length > 0) { toast.error; return }`). Consequence: if monitor-type 404s but silence PUT succeeded, the optimistic silence update is LOST (onSaved never fires, parent state not updated, badge won't appear until the next background refetch). Consider calling onSaved with partial data before the early-return — or include `silencePersisted` and call onSaved with just those fields. This is the user-visible consequence of the backend bug above; fixing the backend resolves it, but a defensive frontend tweak would help.

success_rate.backend: PUT /silence: 100% (200, persists, visible in /api/devices). PUT /monitor-type: 0% on device 192.168.1.3 (still 404). PUT /snmp: not exercised (correctly skipped by dirty-detection).
success_rate.frontend: Steps 1-6: 100% PASS. Step 7 delta (correct set of PUTs): PASS. Step 7 end-to-end: PARTIAL (blocked by residual backend 404 on /monitor-type, already documented).

seed_data_creation: No new seed. Used existing client da3d6e40-b3e5-4d46-9787-dde328a3aa36 / device 192.168.1.3 (id=1089e659-b593-42bf-bb9c-a18aa990e6a9). Cleanup confirmed: alerts_silenced=false, monitor_type=snmp (unchanged — 404 on update side meant DB never moved).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 UX fixes from iteration_65 are confirmed working: (1) DeviceEditModal useEffect re-seed on device prop change, (2) save() dirty-detection gating of the 3 PUTs, (3) ClientOverviewPage optimistic update via setDevices+onSaved. Remaining work is strictly backend: apply the /silence resolver fallback to /monitor-type and /snmp. If you get asked to retest after the backend fix, the expected outcome for STEP 7 is: 2 PUTs both 200, no toast error, badge appears in <1s, modal closes, onSaved fires, optimistic update includes monitor_type change.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: N/A — this iteration validates fixes rather than debugging a new bug. All three iteration_65 mitigations (useEffect re-seed, dirty-detection gating, optimistic update via onSaved) work as designed. The sole remaining anomaly (error toast on STEP 7) is traceable to a KNOWN pre-existing backend bug: PUT /monitor-type and PUT /snmp have not yet received the multi-source resolver fallback that /silence got in iteration_64.
rca_of_the_issue.evidence: Network capture: STEP 3 fires exactly 1 PUT (/silence 200). STEP 4 badge appears 0.51s after modal close. STEP 5 reopen shows silence-toggle.checked=true, silence-reason='Final E2E retest'. STEP 6 fires exactly 1 PUT (/silence 200 with silenced:false), toast 'Alert RIATTIVATI', badge gone in 0.50s. STEP 7 fires exactly 2 PUTs: /monitor-type (404) + /silence (200); /snmp correctly omitted because community was untouched.
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: For the residual STEP 7 error toast: extend the /silence resolver to /monitor-type and /snmp (see action_items). Optional frontend hardening: call onSaved with partial updated fields even when errors.length>0, so silence-only changes surface optimistically even if a sibling PUT fails.
